checks.a_analysis: The output contains no fabricated text. All agenda items, sub-items, and meeting details found in the JSON are present in the source PDF. The AI-generated table captions ('Agenda Topics and Presenters', etc.) are allowed additions.
The phone number (984-204-1487) and Conference ID (350 773 333#) present in the JSON match the source document exactly. No other contact information is present.
Both pages of the source document are represented in the JSON. All agenda items from 1 through 15, including sub-items and the closing 'Next meeting' details, are present.

The agenda structure is captured using a combination of tables and lists to maintain the semantic nesting. Every row in the source's 'Tab/Topic/Presenter' section is accounted for across the various table and list elements in the JSON.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text content is omitted. All fiscal values in sections 4b through 4e are accurately transcribed, and all presenter names are correctly associated with their topics.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are no wording changes that alter the meaning of the document. The text faithfully reflects the source content, including specific details like '(Roll Call Vote Required)'.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly reflects the content of the source document without duplication. Page headers and footers are correctly omitted from the semantic structure, and images appearing on both pages (logos) are represented as distinct image elements, which matches the source document layout.
The heading hierarchy is logical. 'AGENDA' is used as a section title, followed by an H2 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING'. There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.

The agenda, which is a single logical table in the source, has been fragmented into five separate tables in the JSON to accommodate nested lists. While the column count is preserved, tables 2 through 5 (elements 8, 11, 14, and 17) lack the column headers ('Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter') defined at the start of the source document. This failure to propagate or maintain headers across the split table structure is a significant corruption of the semantic organization.
checks.b_evidence_output: The tables starting at element index 8, 11, 14, and 17 do not have header rows ('is_header_row': true).

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the visual flow of the document precisely, moving from the header information through the agenda items in sequence, and finally to the footer information and the second logo.

Lists are generally handled well. Sub-items for the agenda are correctly identified as ordered and unordered lists, including nested items for the Grant Committee Report (item 9a). One minor formatting choice is the flattening of financial data in items 4b-e into single strings, but the textual meaning is preserved.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The heading counts provided in the metadata (JSON: 1, HTML: 3) show a difference of 2. The threshold for failure is the larger of 10% (0.1) or 3 headings. Since the difference of 2 is less than the threshold of 3, this check passes.

The source document contains several clear hyperlinks and contact links that are not captured in the JSON. Specifically, the underlined title 'August 2022 NC 911 Board Meeting' is an explicit link to the agenda packet, and the phone number '984-204-1487' is a standard contact link. These are represented only as plain text or formatted text without 'link' or 'url' attributes.
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding elements in the JSON (element index 2) are paragraphs with text and formatting but no link metadata.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF presents the agenda as a single continuous list/table structure where all items and sub-items are visually nested and associated with their respective presenters. The HTML conversion fragments this into five separate tables ('Agenda Topics and Presenters', 'Continued', 'Continued 2', etc.) and pulls the sub-items out of the table cells, rendering them as standalone lists between the tables. This significantly rearranges the visual flow and dissociates the sub-items from the 'Presenter' column headers, requiring the user to scroll back and forth to maintain context. Furthermore, the numbering/lettering scheme is changed from (a, b, c) to (1, 2, 3) in the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: A single continuous agenda table from Tab 'Call to Order' through item '15', with sub-items (a-f) nested within the 'Topic' column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The agenda is split across five distinct tables with 'Continued' titles. Sub-items for Item 4 and Item 5 are moved out of the table structure into separate numbered lists that break the table flow.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically at Item 4 and Item 5 transitions.

checks.a_analysis: The financial data for NG 911 Reserve Fund, CMRS Account, PSAP Account, and PSAP Grant/Statewide Projects Account (Items 4b-4e in the PDF) is presented in a clearly aligned, tabular format with rows for balances and disbursements. In the HTML output, this data is rendered as continuous, run-on text within single list items, making it impossible to distinguish separate line items or read the values in their intended context. This meets the criteria for 'tables rendered as plain text paragraphs'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 4b-e: NG 911 Reserve Fund, CMRS Account, etc. show aligned rows and columns for 'June 2022 Fund balance', '$ 76,125,411', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 4, points 2-5: 'NG 911 Reserve Fund – June 2022 Fund balance $ 76,125,411 June 2022 Disbursement $ 3,709,069 July 2022 Fund balance $ 76,694,111 July 2022 Disbursement $ 2,419,955'
checks.b_evidence_location: List following the first 'Agenda Topics and Presenters' table.

checks.a_analysis: The 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo, which appears at the bottom of the second page of the source PDF, is completely missing from the HTML output. While the logo appears at the start of the document, its absence at the end is a loss of visual fidelity for the official document's branding.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC DIT 911 Board logo at the bottom of PDF page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML document ends after the 'Next NC 911 Board Meeting...' text and the 'Simultaneous Communication' line; no logo follows.

checks.a_analysis: No visual content duplication was observed between the source and the output.

The document suffers from significant layout fragmentation and tabular data corruption (addressed in V1 and V2); however, no additional degraded quality issues such as excessive whitespace or inconsistent formatting beyond what was already noted were found. The 'Continued' labels are an acceptable way to handle fragmented tables in HTML.
